results.title: The Cold Blob in the Atlantic Just Got a Cause. The Ocean Current That Warms Europe Is Slowing Down.
results.description: A new study by Stefan Rahmstorf and colleagues, published this week in Geophysical Research Letters, concludes that the Atlantic 'cold blob' south of Greenland is being caused by a weakening Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation rather than by surface heat loss. The current that delivers heat to Europe is slowing down, and it is doing it deep enough in the water column to mean what the climate models said it would.

results.title: SpaceX is being fast-tracked into major stock indexes that sit inside retirement accounts and passive funds — meaning millions of ordinary investors may soon own a slice of Elon Musk’s rocket company without ever choosing the stock directly, and perhaps without realizing it.
results.description: SpaceX began trading on the Nasdaq on Friday 12 June under the ticker SPCX, in what was, by the size of the raise, the largest initial public offering on record. The company priced at 135 US dollars a share the evening before, raising roughly 75 billion US dollars at a valuation near 1.77 trillion, and [...] The post SpaceX is being fast-tracked into major stock indexes that sit inside retirement accounts and passive funds — meaning millions of ordinary investors may soon own a slice of Elon Musk’s rocket company without ever choosing the stock directly, and perhaps without realizing it. appeared first on Space Daily .
